summary: First test run for Splash B2B cross-border payment dashboard. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests PASSED covering auth (login/me/cookie), FX rate (live), quote, recipients (seeded 12), transfers (seeded 20 with mixed statuses), stats, transfer creation with 5 stages, advance to completion, batch preview, and validation errors. Frontend: Login → Dashboard redirect works (lands on /), all 4 stat cards render with real data (RM 44,110.05 / 12 recipients / 3 pending / 3m 44s), recent transfers table populated, Quick Actions sidebar present, Recipients page shows all 12 PH names, Transfers page shows SPL refs + Completed/Pending/Failed filters, Batch page has CSV dropzone, Send Payout wizard renders 4 steps (Recipient → Amount → Review → Track) with proper form. No console errors observed.
action_items: Optional: Add data-testid attributes on key UI elements (login-email-input, login-submit-button, nav-*, wizard-next-button, recipient-card, transfer-row, stat-card-*) to make future automation more robust. Currently only generic CSS selectors are usable.
Optional: Sidebar nav links use plain anchors (a href=/send, /transfers etc.) without testids; consider adding for stability.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 491 lines monolithic — consider splitting into routers (auth.py, transfers.py, recipients.py, batch.py) for maintainability if app grows.
FX fetch uses synchronous `requests` inside async endpoint (fetch_fx_rate) — blocks event loop. Use httpx.AsyncClient for non-blocking I/O.
No rate limiting / brute force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends 5-attempt lockout).
calc_quote called inside list_recipients aggregation loop is fine, but list_recipients runs N+1 queries (one aggregate per recipient) — consider single $lookup pipeline.
JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env (acceptable for local dev but flag for prod rotation).
CORS allow_origins falls back to '*' which is incompatible with allow_credentials=True; protected by FRONTEND_URL env being set, but should fail-fast if missing.
advance_transfer mutates stages list and writes whole array; for production prefer atomic $set on a specific index. Acceptable for demo.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on flows tested (login, dashboard, recipients, transfers, batch, send-payout step 1 render)

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 12 PH recipients + 20 transfers (15 completed, 3 pending, 2 failed) for admin user on startup. Tests created/cleaned up TEST_Juan recipient + transfer.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login redirects to / (root), not /dashboard — both render the Dashboard via App.js routing. Bank field on Send Payout step 1 is a custom shadcn-style combobox (not a native <select>); to advance the wizard programmatically use page.click on the trigger then click the option. Other steps (Amount/Review/Track) were not deeply UI-tested but their full backend logic (quote → create transfer → advance through 5 stages → completed) is validated end-to-end by backend_test.py::test_create_recipient_and_transfer_flow. Confetti library (react-confetti) is present in package per request.
